attributes.description: Alerts when buffer cache hit ratio drops below 99%. Cache pressure pushes reads to disk.
attributes.name: [PostgreSQL OTel] Low buffer hit ratio

investigation_guide.blob:
## PostgreSQL low buffer hit ratio

### What fired
`blks_hit / (blks_hit + blks_read) < 99%` with at least 1000 reads.

### Why it matters
Healthy PostgreSQL runs at > 99% hit ratio. Sub-99% indicates shared_buffers pressure or a working-set change.

### Triage
1. Check `shared_buffers` sizing.
2. Inspect recent workload changes.

### Remediation
- Raise `shared_buffers`.
- Tune expensive queries.

### Tuning
- 99% threshold; lower for read-heavy analytic workloads.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-postgresqlreceiver.otel-*
// Buffer hit ratio = blks_hit / (blks_hit + blks_read); healthy is > 99%
| WHERE postgresql.blks_hit IS NOT NULL OR postgresql.blks_read IS NOT NULL
| EVAL hit_val = TO_DOUBLE(postgresql.blks_hit), read_val = TO_DOUBLE(postgresql.blks_read)
| STATS blks_hit = SUM(hit_val), blks_read = SUM(read_val) BY service.instance.id, postgresql.database.name
| WHERE (blks_hit + blks_read) > 1000
// Hit ratio below 99% indicates cache pressure; adjust threshold as needed
| EVAL hit_ratio_pct = ROUND(blks_hit / (blks_hit + blks_read) * 100.0, 2)
| WHERE hit_ratio_pct < 99.0
